preamble: The transformation from a voiced alveolar flap [ɾ] to a voiced alveolar lateral approximant [l]. This liquid shift is often part of a broader liquid merger or dialectal variation.

examples.note: The phoneme /l/ is realized as a flap [ɾ] intervocalically and as a lateral [l] syllable-finally.
Historically related sounds that often interchange in various Philippine languages.

sources: Sohn, H. M. (1999). The Korean Language.
